old_claim.note: Lithium-Ion Batteries (Rechargeable Power) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because handheld mobile phones predate commercial lithium-ion cells and now depend on portable electrochemical batteries instead.

source_shape.source_locator: Nobel Chemistry 2019 summary describing commercial lithium-ion batteries entering the market in 1991.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source anchors commercial lithium-ion batteries to 1991, later than the 1973 handheld mobile-phone milestone.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: That chronology contradicts lithium-ion as a prerequisite for the mobile-phone node while still allowing lithium-ion to improve later phone battery life and scaling.

ontology_before: Mobile phones inherited a late battery-chemistry dependency, making a 1973 handheld phone depend on a 1991 commercial cell technology.
ontology_after: Mobile phones now depend on the broader portable electrochemical battery lineage; lithium-ion can be modeled downstream as a later improvement path.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Graph invariant 2026-06-07-semiconductor-energy-agriculture-chronology.json keeps this removed edge absent and documents the corrected dependency scope.
would_reject_if: The mobile_phones node were narrowed to mean post-1991 lithium-ion-powered mobile phones only.
A stronger source showed the graph should use a separate smartphone-era node for all portable handset dependencies.
A generic rechargeable-battery node replaced portable_electrochemical_batteries and became the better prerequisite.
short_reason: Commercial lithium-ion cells are later than the first handheld mobile phones.
